children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Extract and validate the command field from the input JSON
children.behavior: Parse the input JSON to extract the 'command' field and validate it is one of the allowed commands (send, history, create_channel, join). Return the command and remaining payload.
semantic_inputs: input: Any - JSON with command field
postconditions: Command is one of the allowed values
guarantees: Returns error if command is missing or invalid

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Handle sending a message to a channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the specified channel, then store the message in the messages data store with timestamp and user info.
semantic_inputs: payload: dict - contains channel, user, and message content
semantic_outputs: result: dict - success or error response
preconditions: User is member of the channel
postconditions: Message is stored in messages data store
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel

call_inputs.name: payload
call_inputs.description: Contains channel, user, and message content

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if user is a member of the channel

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Retrieve message history for a channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the channel, then fetch the last 100 messages from the messages data store for that channel, ordered by timestamp.
semantic_inputs: payload: dict - contains channel and user
semantic_outputs: result: dict - list of messages or error
preconditions: User is member of the channel
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check if user is a member of the channel

children.name: CreateChannel
children.behavior: Check that the channel name does not already exist, then create a new channel entry in the channels data store with the creator as the first member.
semantic_inputs: payload: dict - contains channel name and creator user
semantic_outputs: result: dict - success or error response
preconditions: Channel name does not already exist
postconditions: Channel is created and creator is added as member
guarantees: Returns error if channel already exists

call_inputs.name: payload
call_inputs.description: Contains channel name and creator user

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check uniqueness and create new channel entry

children.name: JoinChannel
children.purpose: Add a user to an existing channel
children.behavior: Check that the channel exists and the user is not already a member, then add the user to the channel's member list in the channels data store.
semantic_inputs: payload: dict - contains channel name and user
semantic_outputs: result: dict - success or error response
preconditions: User is not already a member
postconditions: User is added to channel's member list
guarantees: Returns error if channel does not exist or user already member

internal_leaf_accesses.reason: Check existence, check membership, and update member list

dataflow_edges.note: Parent passes raw JSON to ParseCommand
ParseCommand returns command and payload to parent
Parent passes payload to SendMessage when command is 'send'
Parent passes payload to GetHistory when command is 'history'
Parent passes payload to CreateChannel when command is 'create_channel'
Parent passes payload to JoinChannel when command is 'join'

interface_audit.identity_preservation_detail: All five children from Stage 1 are preserved with identical names, purposes, behaviors, boundaries, preconditions, postconditions, guarantees, and composition roles. No children were added, deleted, renamed, or reordered.
interface_audit.semantic_preservation_detail: All Stage 1 semantics (purpose, behavior, boundary, preconditions, postconditions, guarantees, composition_role) are copied verbatim. No modifications were made to any semantic field.
interface_audit.call_signature_detail: Each child's signature contains only the call_inputs parameters. ParseCommand takes 'input' from parent. SendMessage, GetHistory, CreateChannel, JoinChannel each take 'payload' from parent. No internal resources appear in signatures.
interface_audit.internal_leaf_separation_detail: Internal data store accesses (channels, messages) are listed under internal_leaf_accesses and do not appear in call_inputs or signatures. This ensures the parent does not need to pass data store references.
interface_audit.dataflow_schema_detail: All dataflow edges use only 'parent' or exact child names as from_node/to_node. No resource names (raw_data, processed_data, channels, messages, orders, inventory, payments) appear as endpoints.
